event_summary: US-Iran nuclear talks hit a deadlock on 18 May 2026 as Iran declared 'no tangible concessions' in the US response; a drone struck near a UAE nuclear power plant, and Russia floated an Iran-based enrichment consortium proposal.
tldr: With only nine buckets and eleven articles, the headline layer splits cleanly into two frames: POLITICAL (diplomatic deadlock and Trump pressure, four buckets led by China, India, Russia, and the US) and SECURITY_DEFENSE (military fallback scenarios, four buckets — Israel, Pakistan, UK, Ukraine). The clearest headline-layer divergence is between Russia, which frames the talks through sovereignty ('enrichment secures independence') and deal structure, versus Israel, which headlines military inevitability ('Israel acts as if return of war inevitable'). Korea_north is the most isolated bucket (0.570) because its headline is about North Korea denuclearization, not Iran — likely captured in this corpus via the Trump nuclear-deal keyword cluster.

evidence.quote: Chinese, Iranian diplomats meet before US-Iran nuclear talk
Tehran could weigh nuclear deal with US
How Trump's approach with Iran mirrors similar choices
Israel acts as if return of war inevitable
drone attack on UAE nuclear power plant
UAE blames Iran or proxies for strike near nuclear plant
'Escalation Plan' Targeting Iran's Nuclear and Oil Assets
Iran's uranium enrichment secures independence, peaceful atom
US, China to work on denuclearizing North Korea
mirrors similar choices made with North Korea

isolation_top.note: NPR headline uses historical-analogy framing (Iran mirrors North Korea) — analytical register semantically distant from the talks-deadlock news updates in the rest of the corpus.
Dawn leads with the oil market response to the UAE nuclear-plant drone attack — a market-and-security angle diverging from the diplomatic-deadlock dominant.
Kyiv Post headline focuses on US military planning (escalation plan targeting nuclear and oil assets) — operational-military lens distinct from the talks-negotiation majority.
NK News headline is about denuclearizing North Korea, not Iran — captured in this corpus by keyword proximity to the nuclear-deal cluster; topic drift lowers cosine similarity.
Global Times frames the story as Chinese diplomatic support-building for Tehran before talks — a process-and-support angle distinct from the pressure-and-deadlock dominant.

single_outlet_findings.finding: The NK News headline ('US, China to work on denuclearizing North Korea') is about North Korea, not Iran — the clearest evidence of topic bleed from the nuclear-deal keyword cluster into this corpus; Korea_north is in both the SECURITY_DEFENSE and POLICY_PRESCRIPTION frames only via the denuclearization policy analogy.

Only bucket to frame the nuclear talks through a headline asserting Israeli military fatalism ('Israel acts as if return of war inevitable') — the starkest departure from the diplomatic-process framing dominant in all other buckets.

bottom_line: Headlines across the nine-bucket iran_nuclear corpus split cleanly into POLITICAL (deadlock, pressure) versus SECURITY_DEFENSE (military fallback), with Russia uniquely framing the story through sovereignty and deal-structure rather than either. The absence of any ECONOMIC frame at the headline layer — despite the UAE drone-attack oil-price spike visible in Pakistan — is a likely point of headline-body divergence in the sensationalism index.
